message:
fix: use /dev/urandom for salt generation + suppress compiler warnings

password.rs — security improvement:
- generate_salt() now reads from /dev/urandom first for cryptographically
  secure randomness, falling back to the previous entropy-mixing approach
  only when /dev/urandom is unavailable
- Refactored fallback_salt() to use SHA-256 block generation for better
  distribution instead of byte-extracting from DefaultHasher output
- Added thread ID and PID to fallback entropy sources

extract.rs / middleware.rs — compiler warning cleanup:
- Prefix unused variables with underscore (_head, _expected_medium)
- Remove unnecessary mut from Request bindings in ETag middleware tests
  (6 occurrences across etag_middleware_* test functions)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
